## Observed Failure Pattern
All 5 invocations of /systematic-debugging in the 2h window failed with execution_time < 30s (range: 13.4s-32.2s). The skill never reaches Phase 1 — Claude Code exits immediately after model initialization. Error logs show the skill is being invoked but the Claude Code process terminates before any debugging work begins. Tasks affected: 712c61d3 (root cause analysis), 591925d5 (agent triage), 6d15f967 (error spike investigation), 37cc4743 (temujin performance), 0c8fe5e5 (reflection gap audit).

Root cause is likely one of:
1. The skill prompt exceeds context limits when combined with jochi's large CLAUDE.md + memory files
2. Rate limiting cascade — multiple rapid invocations trigger API rate limits
3. The skill requires filesystem access patterns that fail in jochi's sandboxed environment

## Proposed Change to SKILL.md

### Option A — Structural fix (preferred if root cause is phase design)
Add a pre-flight check phase before invoking Claude Code. Verify model availability and rate limit headroom before spawning the debugging session. If pre-flight fails, fall back to a lightweight manual analysis mode that reads logs directly without spawning a full Claude Code session.

### Option B — Scope reduction (if skill is over-broad)
Split /systematic-debugging into two modes: (1) `/debug-triage` — lightweight log analysis that reads error messages and classifies root causes without spawning subagents, and (2) `/debug-deep` — full multi-phase debugging with Claude Code subagent. Jochi defaults to `/debug-triage` for tasks with short expected runtime (<5min). Only escalate to `/debug-deep` for tasks that require code changes.

## Proposed Skill File Change (diff-style)

```diff
## Phase 0 — Pre-flight (new)
+ Before invoking Claude Code, verify:
+ 1. Model endpoint responds (curl health check, 5s timeout)
+ 2. No rate limit headers indicate throttling
+ 3. Task has not already failed 2+ times with <30s execution
+ If any check fails:
+   THEN use fallback mode: read logs with Read tool, classify error, write findings directly
+   DO NOT spawn Claude Code session

## Phase 1 — Reproduce
- Reproduce the issue by running the failing command
+ Reproduce the issue by running the failing command (timeout: 60s per attempt, max 2 attempts)
+ If reproduction fails twice, skip to Phase 3 (Hypothesize) using error message text only
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Phase completion ratio reaches > 0.75 within 5 invocations after change
- No retry_magnet events within 48h of change
- Average execution_time > 60s (indicating actual work is being done)
